_comment: Warhammer franchise data file. Covers Warhammer Fantasy, Warhammer 40,000, and Age of Sigmar across all media.
description: Games Workshop's grimdark fantasy and sci-fi empire. Spans Warhammer Fantasy Battles (1983–2015), Warhammer 40,000 (1987–present), and Age of Sigmar (2015–present). Known for its intricate lore, Gothic aesthetic, and 'grimdark' tone. Massive media presence across video games, novels, and animation.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War
media.description: Seminal RTS that revitalized the 40k license. Focused on cover mechanics and squad-based combat across 4 base races.
media.notes: Includes Winter Assault (2005) and Dark Crusade (2006) expansions. Soulstorm (2008) added Sisters of Battle and Dark Eldar.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War II
media.description: Shifted from RTS base-building to tactical squad RPG. Focused on the Blood Ravens Chapter fighting Tyranids and Chaos.
media.notes: Includes Chaos Rising (2010) and Retribution (2011) expansions.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War III
media.description: Attempted to merge DoW I and II mechanics with MOBA elements. Mixed reception led to cancellation of planned expansions.
media.notes: Final entry in the Dawn of War series to date.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine
media.description: Third-person action game. Captain Titus of the Ultramarines fights Orks and Chaos on Forge World Graia.
media.notes: Cult classic. Sequel announced for 2024/2025.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine II
media.description: Direct sequel to 2011's Space Marine. Captain Titus returns to face the Tyranids and Chaos.
media.notes: Released September 2024.

media.title: Total War: Warhammer
media.description: Total War strategy meets Warhammer Fantasy. The Old World setting with Empire, Dwarfs, Greenskins, and Vampire Counts.
media.notes: First game in the trilogy.

media.title: Total War: Warhammer II
media.description: Expanded the map to Lustria, Ulthuan, and Naggaroth. Introduced High Elves, Dark Elves, Lizardmen, and Skaven.
media.notes: Features Mortal Empires combined map campaign.

media.title: Total War: Warhammer III
media.description: The final entry, set in the Chaos Realms. Adds Kislev, Grand Cathay, and all four Chaos Gods factions.
media.notes: Immortal Empires unites all three games into one massive campaign.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Battlesector
media.description: Turn-based strategy set during the Devastation of Baal. Blood Angels vs Tyranids.
media.notes: Includes expansions for Necrons, Sororitas, and Chaos Daemons.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Mechanicus
media.description: Turn-based tactical RPG following Adeptus Mechanicus Tech-Priests exploring a Necron tomb world.
media.notes: Renowned for its soundtrack and atmosphere. Hereticus DLC adds a new campaign.

media.title: Warhammer: End Times - Vermintide
media.description: Left 4 Dead-style co-op set during the End Times. Ubersreik five fight endless Skaven hordes.
media.notes: Set in the final days of Warhammer Fantasy.

media.title: Warhammer: Vermintide 2
media.description: Co-op melee shooter. Adds Chaos Warriors to the Skaven threat. Expanded career system and loot.
media.notes: Still receiving updates as of 2026. Winds of Magic expansion adds Beastmen.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Darktide
media.description: Spiritual successor to Vermintide set in 40k. Rejects fight cultists and Chaos forces in the hive city Tertium.
media.notes: PC release Nov 2022, Xbox release Oct 2023. PS5 announced for 2024.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Rogue Trader
media.description: Crpg by the Pathfinder developers. Explore the Koronus Expanse as a Rogue Trader. Deep party-based narrative.
media.notes: Released Dec 2023. Void Shadows DLC added 2024.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Boltgun
media.description: Retro boomer shooter. Play as a Sternguard Veteran clearing a Forge World of Chaos.
media.notes: Inspired by Doom and Quake. Highly praised.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Gladius - Relics of War
media.description: 4X strategy game on a single planet. Features Space Marines, Orks, Necrons, and Astra Militarum.
media.notes: Expansions added Chaos, Adeptus Mechanicus, Tyranids, and Tau.

media._comment_game: ── WARHAMMER AGE OF SIGMAR: REALM OF RUIN ────────────────
media.title: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Realms of Ruin
media.description: RTS set in Age of Sigmar. Stormcast Eternals vs Orruk Kruleboyz vs Nighthaunt.
media.notes: Released Nov 2023. Underperformed commercially.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Shootas, Blood & Teef
media.description: 2D side-scrolling run-and-gun. Play as an Ork Warboss to retrieve your stolen Squig from Chaos.
media.notes: Metal soundtrack.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Inquisitor - Martyr
media.description: Diablo-style ARPG. Play as a Caligari Sector Inquisitor purging heretics, xenos, and daemons.
media.notes: Prophecy expansion adds Tech-Adept class.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Freebooter
media.description: Defunct mobile RPG. Ork Freebooterz searching for loot. DEFUNCT.
media.notes: Removed from stores. No longer playable.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Lost Crusade
media.description: Defunct MMO strategy game. Build your chapter and fight across the galaxy.
media.notes: Removed from stores.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Combat Cards
media.description: Mobile card game. Collect and upgrade units from across 40k factions. Still live.
media.notes: Active as of 2026.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Tacticus
media.description: Turn-based tactical mobile RPG. Collectible characters across multiple factions. Very successful.
media.notes: Active as of 2026.

media._comment_game: ── WARHAMMER AGE OF SIGMAR: STORM GROUND ─────────────────
media.title: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Storm Ground
media.description: Turn-based strategy set in the Mortal Realms. Three factions: Stormcast, Nighthaunt, Maggotkin.
media.notes: Roguelite campaign structure.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Angels of Death
media.description: CG animation on Warhammer+. Follows the Blood Angels Chapter during the Cryptus Campaign.
media.notes: Warhammer+ exclusive. Voice acting from the gaming community.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Hammer and Bolter
media.description: Animated anthology series. Each episode explores a different faction or event in 40k.
media.notes: Warhammer+ exclusive. Varied animation quality and tones.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Pariah Nexus
media.description: CG animated series. Ultramarines, Sisters of Battle, and Sororitas trapped in the Necron Pariah Nexus.
media.notes: Warhammer+ exclusive. Visually impressive.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: The Exodite
media.description: CG series following a Tau Stealth Team infiltrating a world under Imperial and Chaos influence.
media.notes: Warhammer+ exclusive. Released weekly over three weeks.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Iron Within
media.description: Warhammer+ animated short. Chaos Marines of the Iron Warriors face a planetary uprising.
media.notes: Standalone 40-minute special.

media.title: Astartes
media.description: Fan-made animated short film series. Highly detailed depiction of Retributor Chapter Space Marines. Acquired by Games Workshop and made official.
media.notes: Originally YouTube. Now Warhammer+ exclusive. Defining piece of fan animation. Prestige fan work.

media.title: Ultramarines: A Warhammer 40,000 Movie
media.description: Direct-to-DVD CGI film. Ultramarines investigate a distress signal on a Chaos-corrupted world.
media.notes: Mixed reception. Voice cast includes John Hurt, Terence Stamp, Sean Pertwee.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: The Motion Picture
media.description: Canceled CGI film by Fanderson/SAB. First attempt at a Warhammer feature film. Only trailer and behind-the-scenes footage survive.
media.notes: Canceled. Lost media with surviving footage. Obtained rights but never completed.

media.title: Secret Level (Warhammer 40,000 Episode)
media.description: Live-action/animated anthology series from the creators of Love, Death & Robots. Features a stand-alone 40k episode. Directed by Dave Wilson.
media.notes: Released Dec 2024. Henry Cavill executive producer.

media.title: Eisenhorn (TV Series)
media.description: Canceled live-action adaptation of Dan Abnett's Eisenhorn novels. Was in development at the BBC but shelved.
media.notes: Canceled. Only concept art exists.

media.title: The Horus Heresy Audio Dramas
media.description: Full-cast audio dramas set during the Horus Heresy. Includes: Garro, Raven's Flight, The Burden of Loyalty, and many more.
media.notes: Produced by Black Library. Treated as canonical media. Separate from standard audiobook narrations.

media.title: The Horus Heresy (Novel Series)
media.author: Various (Dan Abnett, Graham McNeill, Aaron Dembski-Bowden, etc.)
media.description: Foundational 60+ novel series detailing the civil war that shattered the Imperium. The defining literary work of 40k.
media.notes: Published 2006–2024. The End and the Death (Vol 1-3) concluded the series in 2024.

media.title: Gaunt's Ghosts
media.author: Dan Abnett
media.description: Long-running series following the Tanith First-and-Only Imperial Guard regiment. Sharpe in space.
media.notes: First novel First and Only (1999). 16+ novels as of 2026.

media.title: Eisenhorn
media.author: Dan Abnett
media.description: Inquisitor Gregor Eisenhorn trilogy. Xenos, Malleus, Hereticus. Cornerstone 40k literature.
media.notes: Followed by Ravenor and Bequin series.

media.title: Ciaphas Cain
media.author: Sandy Mitchell
media.description: Comedic series. Memoirs of a self-proclaimed cowardly Commissar. 10+ novels.
media.notes: Tone contrasts with the grimdark setting.

media.title: Gotrek & Felix
media.author: William King, Nathan Long, etc.
media.description: Long-running Warhammer Fantasy series. Slayer Gotrek and poet Felix Jaeger travel the Old World.
media.notes: First novel Trollslayer (1999). Influential to the setting. Now continues in Age of Sigmar.

media._comment_book: ── BLACK LIBARY THE END TIMES SERIES (FANTASY) ────────────
media.title: The End Times
media.author: Various
media.description: Five-part series chronicling the destruction of the Warhammer Fantasy world and transition to Age of Sigmar.
media.notes: Published 2014–2015. Controversial among fans.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000 Codex Series
media.author: Games Workshop
media.description: Core rulebooks and faction codices. The primary source of lore for the setting, beyond the novels.
media.notes: 10th Edition released 2023. Each faction has a codex with extensive background.

media.title: Warhammer Fantasy Roleplay
media.description: TTRPG set in the Old World. 4th Edition published by Cubicle 7.
media.notes: Not a digital game, but a foundational media piece. Included as a 'substantial board game.'

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Rogue Trader
media.description: The original 40k rulebook. More RPG than wargame, establishing the core lore.
media.notes: 1st Edition of Warhammer 40,000. Treat as foundational media.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Inquisitor
media.description: Skirmish-level wargame focused on Inquisitorial retinues. 54mm scale.
media.notes: Out of print. Precursor to Inquisitor: Martyr video game.

media.title: Space Hulk
media.description: Turn-based strategy. Terminators vs Genestealers inside a Space Hulk.
media.notes: Multiple versions including 1995, 2013 remake, and Tactics (2018).

media.title: Warhammer: Mark of Chaos
media.description: RTS set in Warhammer Fantasy. Empire vs Chaos.
media.notes: Battle March expansion added Orcs and Dark Elves.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Fire Warrior
media.description: First-person shooter. Play as a Tau Fire Warrior fighting Imperials, Orks, and Chaos.
media.notes: Mixed reception. PS2 version has a unique soundtrack.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Final Liberation
media.description: Turn-based strategy. Epic scale game featuring Imperial Guard vs Orks on Armageddon.
media.notes: Full motion video cutscenes.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Chaos Gate
media.description: Turn-based strategy. Grey Knights vs Chaos Space Marines.
media.notes: Spiritual successor released 2022: Chaos Gate - Daemonhunters.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Chaos Gate - Daemonhunters
media.description: Modern turn-based strategy sequel/remake. Grey Knights vs Nurgle's forces.
media.notes: Highly praised. Duty Eternal expansion adds new units.

media.title: Battlefleet Gothic: Armada
media.description: Real-time strategy space battles. Imperium vs Chaos, Orks, Eldar.
media.notes: Set in the Gothic Sector.

media.title: Battlefleet Gothic: Armada II
media.description: Sequel. Larger scale, includes Necrons, Tyranids, Tau, and a campaign focused on the Gathering Storm.
media.notes: Definitive 40k space game.

media.title: Necromunda: Hired Gun
media.description: Fast-paced FPS set in the Underhive. Bounty hunter gameplay with grappling hook.
media.notes: Preceded by Necromunda: Underhive Wars (2020), a tactical game.

media.title: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Champions
media.description: Digital card game with physical tie-ins. Servers closed in 2022. DEFUNCT.
media.notes: No longer playable.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Lost Crusade
media.description: Defunct MMO strategy game. Build your chapter and fight across the galaxy.
media.notes: Removed from stores.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Space Wolf
media.description: Turn-based card game RPG. Space Wolves vs Chaos on a planet.
media.notes: Still available.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Dark Nexus Arena
media.description: Canceled MOBA. Steam early access never completed. DEFUNCT.
media.notes: Canceled during Early Access.

media.title: Warhammer: Vermintide - The End Times
media.description: Mobile companion app for Vermintide. Mini-game and lore collection. DEFUNCT.
media.notes: Removed from stores.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Damnation Crusade
media.description: Graphic novel. Follows a Black Templar initiate from recruitment to death. Published by Boom! Studios.
media.notes: Out of print. Predecessor to Titan Comics' current license.

media.title: Marneus Calgar
media.description: Marvel Comics miniseries (5 issues). Origin story of the Ultramarines Chapter Master.
media.notes: Published by Marvel. Kieron Gillen and Jacen Burrows.

media.title: Sister of Battle
media.description: Marvel Comics miniseries (5 issues). Follows a Battle Sister on a sacred mission.
media.notes: Published by Marvel. Torunn Grønbekk and Edgar Salazar.

media.title: Blood Angels
media.description: Graphic novel series by Boom! Studios. 5-issue miniseries covering the Blood Angels Chapter.
media.notes: Out of print.

media.title: Forge of War
media.description: Marvel Comics one-shot. Set during the Horus Heresy. Focus on Ferrus Manus.
media.notes: Published by Marvel.
